lines.text: Welcome back. The clock reads {{CLOCK}}. We are {{ELAPSED}} into the night.
[pause] Breathe in through your nose. Four counts. [pause] Hold.
Release through your mouth. Slowly. There is no rush.
[pause] Some of you are in the West Side tonight. Monroe Street. Nakoma. Hilldale.
Some of you live in houses where the locks are very good. [pause] That does not make you safe. That makes you aware you are not safe.
Breathe in. [pause] The awareness is the first step.
[pause] I want you to picture the room you are in. The walls. The color of the walls.
Now picture the people you love in other rooms. Doors between you. [pause] Closed doors.
The doors are not keeping them safe. The doors are keeping you honest about what safety means.
[pause] Breathe.
Release. [pause] There are sounds outside. You may hear them or you may not. Either way, they exist.
The ones making the sounds are not your responsibility. Say that to yourself. [pause] Not your responsibility.
Breathe in. [pause] The people on Monroe Street tonight — some of them are choosing. Choosing to stay. Choosing to listen.
Choosing to not open the door. [pause] That is a form of love.
Breathe. [pause] Release.
Now I want you to think about a person you have not forgiven. [pause] Not someone you hate. Someone you have simply decided will not enter your forgiveness.
Can you see their face? [pause] You do not have to forgive them tonight.
But you can release the energy of holding their face. [pause] Release it with each exhale.
Ka-semala-dith. [pause] Release.
[pause] Breathe in. The night is two-thirds through. We are in the deepest part. The part where things are quieter.
Or louder. [pause] Depending on where you are.
The Order teaches that silence and sound are the same thing. [pause] Both are the world telling you something.
Listen to what your silence is telling you.
[pause] Breathe. In. [pause] Four. [pause] Hold.
There is a woman on Nakoma Drive who is standing at her kitchen window. She has been standing there for two hours.
[pause] I do not know her name. [pause] But I know her breathing.
She is breathing like you are breathing right now. [pause] Slow. Deliberate. Choosing each one.
That is connection. [pause] That is all connection is.
Release. [pause] Let your shoulders fall.
The tension in your shoulders is the weight of decisions you made before the clock struck seven. [pause] You chose to stay. You chose to listen.
That was the decision. [pause] Everything after that is just breathing.
[pause] Breathe in. [pause] Four. [pause] Hold.
The Purge does not ask questions. [pause] The Purge simply is.
And you are still here. [pause] Still breathing. Still choosing the light of this radio over the dark outside.
Release. [pause] That is grace.
[pause] Some of you are in Hilldale. Protected neighborhoods. Expensive doors. Security systems that cost more than cars.
And some of you are in places where the doors are old. [pause] Where the locks are familiar with the sound of breaking.
Both of you are breathing the same air tonight. [pause] Both of you are afraid in different shapes.
Breathe in. [pause] Your fear is not a failure. It is evidence that you understand what is happening.
Release it. [pause] Not the understanding. [pause] The fear.
[pause] We are {{ELAPSED}} into the night. [pause] Four hours remain.
Four hours is enough time to go very far. [pause] Or to stay very still.
Choose still. [pause] Choose the breath.
In. [pause] Four. [pause] Hold.
The Sisters teach that the Purge is a mirror. [pause] What you see in it is what you have become.
But mirrors can crack. [pause] And when they crack, sometimes you see something else.
[pause] Release. [pause] That is enough for now.
Stay with us. [pause] Stay with your breath.
The sun is not far away. [pause] And you will see it.

tickerItems.text: West Side (Nakoma / Monroe / Hilldale) — activity up 8% since 02:00. Pattern suggests coordinated movement from Capitol Square. Police scanner silent.
{{ELAPSED}} checkpoint: Madison-wide estimate 159 cumulative. East Side plateau holding. West Side spike accelerating.
The ones outside are not your responsibility. The ones inside are the only ones that matter.
Lake Mendota shoreline: volunteer recovery team still searching. No additional bodies recovered since 01:10.
